purpose: PostToolUse hook on Write. When a worker writes to a project file that has an entry in the PKI manifest ({project_root}/.synapse/knowledge/manifest.json), atomically marks that entry's `stale=true` and recomputes `stats.stale` count via jq + write-then-rename. This is the staleness signal that drives `!learn_update` — the PKI knows which annotations need refreshing because workers' writes flag them automatically. Skips Synapse internal progress files (dashboards/*/progress/*) and anything under .synapse/knowledge/ (prevents infinite loops where annotation writes mark themselves stale).
gotchas: Skip guards for .synapse/knowledge/ are essential — without them, writing the manifest would mark itself stale recursively
Only updates if entry exists AND is not already stale — idempotent
Atomic update via .tmp.$$ + mv — partial writes never visible
Silent on all errors (exit 0) — never blocks the agent's work; PKI staleness is a best-effort signal
patterns: PostToolUse atomic JSON manifest update with write-then-rename idiom
PKI integration: feeds .synapse/knowledge/ manifest from worker tool calls
conventions: TMP_MANIFEST=${MANIFEST}.tmp.$$ uses PID for collision safety
